macro_events.event_summary: Trump announced US-Iran talks will resume in Pakistan on Monday, signaling potential de-escalation though Iran retains control of the Strait of Hormuz.
primary_drivers: Reduced Middle East geopolitical risk premium in oil
Potential pathway to Iranian crude returning to global market if sanctions ease
Lower tail risk of Strait of Hormuz disruption

beneficiaries.rationale: Jet fuel is ~20-25% of operating costs; lower crude price from reduced Iran risk premium directly expands margins. Delta is least hedged among majors, maximizing spot price sensitivity.
Same jet fuel cost exposure as Delta; carries large international long-haul exposure that also benefits from Middle East de-escalation reducing reroute risk.
Domestic carrier with highest fuel cost as % of opex; unhedged spot exposure means any crude pullback on de-escalation flows straight to margin.
Cruise line bunker fuel sensitivity plus Mediterranean/Middle East itinerary risk reduction; dual benefit from lower oil and reduced regional geopolitical concerns for routing.

losers.rationale: Integrated major with highest earnings leverage to Brent among US supermajors; a de-escalation that removes Iran risk premium compresses near-term oil prices and E&P segment earnings.
Pure-play US E&P with high beta to WTI and leveraged balance sheet; falls hardest on any sustained crude pullback from diplomatic progress.
Small-cap E&P with outsized oil price sensitivity; also has Egypt operations exposing it specifically to Middle East narrative flows.
Defense prime with heavy Middle East FMS exposure (PAC-3, THAAD, F-35 to regional partners); de-escalation narrative pressures defense multiples even without near-term order impact.
Patriot/missile defense demand thesis partly built on Iran threat; any diplomatic thaw headline creates sentiment-driven unwind in defense basket.
Oilfield services revenue tied to upstream capex decisions, which track oil price; sustained crude weakness from Iran deal prospects pressures activity outlook.

headlines: Trump says talks between U.S. and Iran to resume in Pakistan on Monday
